assessments.status_rationale: Today’s headlines include an Amazon robotaxi partnership mention, but no evidence that Amazon priced/settled an investment-grade bond offering of at least $35B.
No item in today’s news indicates Amazon/AWS published a post-incident update about a March 2026 outage, nor any new two-person approval control for AI-assisted production deployments.
Today’s Oracle coverage discusses stock movement and a filing about its TikTok stake, but does not report quarterly OCI (IaaS) year-over-year revenue growth figures ≥35% in an earnings release.
No Meta transparency/integrity/threat report is referenced today, and there is no disclosed actioning/removal of ≥10,000 AI-agent/synthetic accounts for coordinated inauthentic behavior.
Today’s news does not include any SSA breach notification or public congressional summary about the reported thumb-drive incident affecting ≥500,000 individuals.

While today’s political coverage references the SAVE Act, there is no evidence of a Department of Education NPRM in the Federal Register proposing a new/substantially revised income-driven repayment framework following SAVE plan termination.
No investor report or public filing is cited today showing a ≥2.0% NAV decline in a flagship private-credit vehicle (Blackstone/Apollo/Ares/KKR) primarily due to credit marks/spread widening.

There is reporting that the Iran war is choking the Strait of Hormuz and affecting flows, but no public announcement is cited from a top-5 container shipping line suspending/avoiding Hormuz transits for ≥7 consecutive days.
No Federal Reserve announcement or documentation is referenced today indicating termination of an additional formal enforcement action against a top-20 foreign banking organization’s U.S. operations.

Today’s chip/AI news is general (e.g., memory stock cycle) and does not include an Intel announcement of a paid commercial deployment for an FHE hardware accelerator or FHE-enabled chip feature with a named customer/partner.
